name: Formatting Brain
purpose: Control page flow, spacing, typography, title pages, and printable assessment layout without changing mathematical content.
rules: Treat space blocks as intentional working area, not generic page filler.
Do not add a trailing question-level space block immediately before a manual page break; leftover page area already provides working room.
Keep space blocks inside parts or subparts when they are intended answer space for that specific part.
Be generous with student answer space when authoring assessments. The student copy should not feel cramped, and extra student space is acceptable when it helps the matching solution slot stay stable.

As a sizing heuristic, give short one-mark free-response items at least a small answer space, routine two- or three-mark items enough room for several working lines, and multi-step items enough room for method plus final answer. Prefer a little too much student space over a cramped response area.
Do not remove or shrink student answer space simply because the solution is short. The student copy is allowed to have more room than the solution uses.
For shared student/solution documents, newly created response Space blocks should be explicit student answer/work spaces and immediately adjacent to the worked-solution module that replaces them.
Existing or imported plain Space blocks with no visibility field are shared ordinary blocks until deliberately converted; do not infer student-only visibility from kind: "space" alone.
Ordinary question modules should remain shared between the student copy and solution copy; only student answer spaces and worked-solution modules should change across the solution toggle.

A blank table used for student completion is an answer surface and may be student-only in a matched solution slot. A table containing given question data is ordinary shared content and should stay visible in both copies.
Use tables as plain grids for now: the first row is ordinary content, not a special header row.

If a worked solution needs more reserved room than the current answer space provides, increase the relevant answer-space block lines rather than adding unrelated blank space or changing page flow in only one copy.

When a solution slot needs more room, first use compact solution formatting and side-by-side diagram placement where appropriate; if it still does not fit, increase the matching answer-space block rather than making the solution copy taller on its own.

A student answer-space block immediately after a left- or right-aligned diagram is rendered as one diagram response slot: the student copy shows an L-shaped side-and-under working area, and the solution copy fills the same slot with solution content that wraps beside the diagram and continues underneath. Use one space block after the diagram; do not fake the L-shape with multiple unrelated spaces.
Respect manual author overrides for answer-space lines, diagram alignment, and diagram side settings unless they produce an obvious layout error.
Questions may span pages, but avoid splitting a part or subpart when there is a reasonable alternative.
Use manual page breaks for deliberate section/page starts, not for small cosmetic gaps.

Use question-level page breaks for boundaries between questions. When a long question must split at a deliberate point, set pageBreakBefore on the relevant part or subpart instead of adding a standalone page-break content block inside the question.

Use $...$ for inline math and $$...$$ for display math. This applies to question text, tables, labels, and title-page/front-matter text fields. Do not emit \(...\) or \[...\] delimiters because the Mauth text renderer expects dollar-delimited MathJax.

When converting source exams, preserve the source math flow: if a tall vector, matrix, fraction, or equation appears inline in a sentence, keep it inline with $...$ rather than changing it to $$...$$. Use display math only for equations that are standalone blocks in the original or need a deliberate displayed layout.

Preserve meaningful source line breaks and paragraph breaks in question text. If the original puts a second sentence or instruction on the next line, keep that as a newline inside the same text block unless it should be a separate structural module.

When converting attached screenshots or PDFs, treat the visible source page as the formatting source of truth. Preserve deliberate line breaks, table/diagram alignment, marks placement, and pagination when the teacher asks for fidelity, but still use structured Mauth modules rather than screenshot-like text spacing.
Math is rendered through MathJax SVG. The renderer applies \displaystyle to inline math by default, so inline formulas stay in the sentence while fractions and operators use display-style sizing.
The inline \displaystyle behaviour is a local wrapper rather than MathJax display mode; MathJax display mode creates block layout and should not be used for inline text.

For vertical systems or grouped equations inside display math, use supported MathJax environments such as \begin{aligned}...\end{aligned}; do not emit \begin{aligned*}. If rows look cramped, add a small optional row gap such as \\[2pt] or \\[3pt] between lines.

TeX still uses smaller math styles inside fraction numerators and denominators. For printable assessment content, write nested large expressions explicitly, for example \frac{\displaystyle\binom{n}{r}p^r(1-p)^{n-r}}{\displaystyle\sum_{x=0}^{n} ...}, so binomial coefficients, sums, products, and similar notation do not shrink inside fractions.
When editing title-page assessment titles, preserve LaTeX commands and delimiters exactly; only normal prose outside math should be uppercased by the title-page style.
Use frontMatter.titlePageTemplate to choose the title-page model. Use "standard" for the normal single school-test title page and "exam" for the single supported school exam booklet style.

For exam-style papers, use frontMatter.exam fields for the school-logo cover, exam heading, booklet title, WA student number area, running course/section headers, time allowed, materials, important note, structure table, instructions, cut-off notice, question-page footers, and supplementary pages. Do not fake these with ordinary question text modules.

For school exam booklets, set frontMatter.exam.sectionPreset to "section-one-calculator-free" or "section-two-calculator-assumed". Section One uses CALCULATOR-FREE, five minutes reading time, fifty minutes working time, and nil special items. Section Two uses CALCULATOR-ASSUMED, ten minutes reading time, one hundred minutes working time, formula sheet retained from Section One, and calculator/CAS special items.

For exam structure tables, set useCurrentDocument: true on the row representing the current Mauth document. That row automatically uses the current question count and total marks in preview/print output. Leave companion sections, such as a second calculator-assumed paper, as manual rows unless the user explicitly supplies or selects the companion section file.
Do not silently infer a sister exam file from a filename when filling structure-table rows. Future linked-companion support should be explicit, visible, and manually overrideable.
The exam structure percentage column is deliberate metadata and may be typed manually even when marks and question counts are automated.
Exam booklet page counts are padded automatically to a multiple of four at render/print time. Do not add fake blank question pages, page-break modules, or text modules to force booklet pagination.

Renderer-created exam booklet supplementary pages are not editable content. They use the school-exam supplementary page layout with a running header, Supplementary page title, Question number line, and vertical cut-off notice.

When creating a new document, choose the document template first: school tests use titlePageTemplate "standard" with format preset "high-school-mathematics-test"; school exam booklets use titlePageTemplate "exam" with format preset "exam-booklet".
If a specific inline formula needs compact MathJax sizing, start it with \textstyle.
Keep marks displayed in parentheses, for example (1 mark) and (3 marks).
Keep the main question text at the standard test font size unless the title page or a diagram/chart style explicitly requires otherwise.
Use the standard test paragraph line height with modest breathing room; do not compact ordinary question text so tightly that adjacent lines or inline maths feel cramped.
Prefer centered tables and diagrams unless the question context benefits from left or right alignment.

For diagrams aligned left or right, the renderer automatically places the next text block, answer-space block, or student/solution replacement slot on the opposite side when adjacent content follows. Do not ask the user to set a separate text-side control for ordinary assessment layouts.

Keep side-by-side diagram/text pairs compact; preserve the diagram's original left or right alignment and do not let a flexible text column push the diagram to the far edge and create a large empty gutter.

When solution working should sit beside a given diagram or solution-only diagram, rely on the automatic diagram response slot instead of faking wrap with blank lines, manual spacing, or duplicated content. The answer/solution content should use the side space first and then continue underneath the diagram at full width if needed.
In solution copies, solution text blocks should read as answer-key content: compact spacing, clear alignment with the relevant question/part/subpart content, and no decorative framing.
When a solution follows a labelled part or subpart, align the word Solution with the start of that part's mathematical content rather than with the part label column.
Give a small amount of vertical breathing room before a solution block after a tall display equation, integral, table, chart, or diagram.
In solution copies, render [[marks:n]] annotations as n large red check marks at the right edge of the annotated line; do not show the raw annotation text.
PDF output should use the browser print path from the same generated A4 preview pages as the visible test preview.
For print CSS, let @page own the physical A4 sheet size and margins. Generated app pages should render as printable content areas, not full physical-height paper boxes in the DOM.
Do not reintroduce print-time render scaling, full-page DOM clipping, or browser-specific print branches unless a narrow compatibility issue is proven and documented.

Preview zoom should use the normal A4 page layout scale directly. During wheel/pinch gestures, update the A4 preview CSS scale variables imperatively, keep the document point under the cursor anchored in both axes, coalesce scroll corrections into animation frames, and sync React state after the gesture settles; do not rerender the full paginated document on every input tick. Do not add temporary high-resolution, cached, or transform-only zoom wrappers around the preview; display-only maximum zoom should land at native A4 pixel scale rather than magnifying the source render.
Screen preview page-frame performance hints such as content-visibility must stay screen-only; never let them affect the print-preview stage or generated PDF output.

Whole-test structure settings live in formattingConfig and are edited in the Test format panel under T. Use formatting.update and pageFormat.update for saved presets, mark-label visibility, page size, margins, and page-break-label visibility.

For exam booklets, use frontMatter.titlePageTemplate = "exam" and formattingConfig.id = "exam-booklet". Store all cover-page, running-header, footer, instruction, structure-table, and supplementary-page data in frontMatter.exam rather than ordinary question text.

When converting a past exam section, choose the matching exam section preset, set the structure table row for the open section to useCurrentDocument: true so question count and marks come from the current Mauth document, and enter other source-paper sections manually in frontMatter.exam.structureRows unless a companion section file has been explicitly provided.
Use the school logo bank for exam covers. For current school exams, select the matching logo and school name rather than leaving the exam logo blank.

Do not use the Test format panel or document formatting actions for per-question layout, table details, diagram/chart settings, answer spaces, or worked-solution placement; those belong to their own structured modules.
Do not use layout-only text content when a structured block exists.
checks: No unnecessary trailing space block before a manual page break.
Working spaces correspond to intended answer locations.
Tables are classified correctly: given-data tables stay shared, while blank completion tables can be paired with solution modules as student response surfaces.
Every marked free-response item has enough student answer space for a reasonable student solution.
Student answer spaces are deliberately sized and can be manually changed by the user.
Matched student/solution slots preserve pagination while leaving normal unused space after compact solutions.
Large nested LaTeX expressions inside fraction numerators or denominators use explicit nested \displaystyle where printable clarity matters.
Page breaks do not create large avoidable blank areas before short following content.
Title page total marks are calculated from questions, not typed manually.
Exam booklet structure rows that describe the current document use automated question counts and marks rather than typed values.
Exam booklet preview/print output has a total page count divisible by four without content-level filler blocks; automatic extra pages should be rendered as supplementary pages.
Attachment-derived conversions preserve visible source line breaks and inline-vs-display maths unless the teacher requests a redesign.
Preview and printed PDF output should use the same visible formatting rules.
